content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

December 4, 1991

Margie Weggeland  
920 Via Cartago, #52  
Riverside, CA 92507

Re: Followup to Your Request for Informal Assistance  
Our File No. I-91-160a

Dear Ms. Weggeland:

On March 20, 1991, you were advised that the Political Reform Act did not prohibit you, as an administrative assistant to Assemblymember Kelly, from participating on your own time as an officer or consultant of your family's consulting business. (Weggeland Advice Letter, No. I-91-160, copy enclosed.) A typographical error was included on page two of the letter. Our prior advice remains unchanged: we write this letter to correct the relevant portions of Government Code Section 89502 that are quoted with extraneous language on page two. Subparagraph (c) should read as follows, with the extraneous language in strike out type:

(c) "Honorarium" does not include earned income for personal services which are customarily provided in connection with the individual's business or practice of a bona fide business, trade, or profession, such as teaching, practicing law, medicine, insurance, real estate, banking, or building contracting, unless the sole or predominant activity of the business, trade, or profession is making speeches.

Please contact me if you have any questions.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin  
Acting General Counsel

By: Jonathan S. Rothman  
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JSR:ken  
Enclosure
